name: Estonia
contextBlurb: Estonia implements the EU’s MiCA regime through its Markets in Crypto-Assets Act, making MiCA directly applicable and appointing Finantsinspektsioon as the national competent authority for authorising and supervising CASPs and stablecoin issuers. Under MiCA, single-currency e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) require prior authorisation, governance and reserve safeguards; EMTs must offer redemption at par value at all times. Algorithmic tokens are not recognised as ART/EMT and cannot be marketed as “stable.” Estonia applies the EU transitional (“grandfathering”) regime—existing CASPs can operate while seeking authorisation for up to 18 months.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
EU DAC8 crypto-asset reporting rules start applying; Danish providers must report to tax authorities.
Eurodollar ApS announces MiCA authorisation as Denmark’s first EMT issuer.
Remaining MiCA provisions take effect and the optional 18-month transitional (“grandfathering”) period for existing CASPs begins.
MiCA Titles III–IV (stablecoin rules for ARTs/EMTs) enter into application across the EU, including Denmark.

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Estonia implements the EU’s MiCA regime through its Markets in Crypto-Assets Act, making MiCA directly applicable and appointing Finantsinspektsioon as the national competent authority for authorising and supervising CASPs and stablecoin issuers. Under MiCA, single-currency e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) require prior authorisation, governance and reserve safeguards; EMTs must offer redemption at par value at all times. Algorithmic tokens are not recognised as ART/EMT and cannot be marketed as “stable.” Estonia applies the EU transitional (“grandfathering”) regime—existing CASPs can operate while seeking authorisation for up to 18 months.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: In Estonia, stablecoins fall under the EU’s MiCA as either Electronic Money Tokens (EMTs) or Asset-Referenced Tokens (ARTs). regulators.name: Estonian Financial Supervision and Resolution Authority
regulators.role: National competent authority for MiCA. Licenses and supervises crypto-asset service providers and any MiCA-compliant stablecoin issuers in Estonia, enforcing capital, governance, reserve, disclosure a

regulators.name: Financial Intelligence Unit
regulators.role: Historically oversaw AML/CFT compliance and issued crypto licenses. Now focuses on suspicious transaction reporting, with full licensing authority transitioning to EFSA by July 1, 2026.
